subject: Chemistry
title: Chadwick Finds the Neutron
description: A quick win: understand Chadwick Finds the Neutron.

hook.text: For 20 years, physicists knew atoms were too heavy for just protons and electrons. Something was missing.

buildup.text: Helium has an atomic mass of 4 but only 2 protons. What accounts for the other half of its mass?

discovery.text: In 1932, James Chadwick bombarded beryllium with alpha particles and detected a new particle with no charge—the neutron.

twist.text: Neutrons have almost the same mass as protons. They hold the nucleus together despite protons repelling each other.

climax.text: The neutron made nuclear fission possible. Without charge, neutrons penetrate nuclei easily—triggering chain reactions.

punchline.text: No charge, nearly invisible. The neutral particle that completed the atom.

quiz.question: Why was discovering the neutron so important?
options: Neutrons have no charge, letting them penetrate nuclei and trigger fission
Neutrons proved that atoms can be destroyed
Neutrons showed that protons are not fundamental particles
